title: koeleria oreophila subsp. rosei
intro: Koeleria oreophila subsp. rosei, also known as Rose's Koeleria, is a sub-species of the Koeleria oreophila plant, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, Koeleria oreophila subsp. rosei has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to possess health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various diseases. In traditional herbal medicine, Koeleria oreophila subsp. rosei is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
